content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Bruce Pointkowski 
Legislative Consultant 
Assemblyman Ted Lempert 
state Capitol 
November 21, 1989 
P.o. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Dear Mr. Pointkowski: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-652 
This is in response to your request for confirmation of 
telephone advice regarding the mass mailing restrictions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act") .1/ This letter confirms that the 
sample of the mass mailing (copy attached) submitted with your 
request for confirmation does not violate the mass mailing provi-
sions of the Act. 
As I stated in our telephone conversation, section 89001 
provides that no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at 
public expense. 
The Act defines a "mass mailing" as more than 200 
substantially similar pieces of mail sent in a calendar month, but 
excludes mailings sent in response to a specific, unsolicited 
request. 
(Section 82041.5; Regulation 18901(d), copy enclosed.) 
Regulation 18901(c) provides that a newsletter or other mass mail-
ing may not be sent within the meaning of section 89001 if the 
name, office or other reference to an elected officer or his or 
her photograph appears on the document. 
There are certain 
exceptions to this general rule. 
(See Regulation 18901(e) and 
(f) • ) 
The mailing you have submitted includes Assemblyman Lempert's 
name and title in the standard letterhead of the Assemblyman's 
office and in no other place in the mailing. 
Regulation 18901(e) 
provides that a newsletter or other mass mailing is not prohibited 
by section 89001 if the stationery, forms and envelopes used for 
the mailing are the standard stationary, forms and envelopes of 
1 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322,5660 


File No. A-89-652 
Page 2 
the agency or committee of the agency, and the name of the elected 
officer appears in the standard letterhead of the stationery, 
forms or envelopes of the agency. 
Your mailing falls within this 
exception. 
Please note that the Commission is in the process of revising 
Regulation 18901, which implements the statutory mass mailing 
restrictions. 
We anticipate discussion of the amendments at the 
December 12, 1989 Commission meeting, and adoption of the 
amendments at the December 13, 1989 Commission meeting. 
A draft 
of the proposed amendments is enclosed. 
Nevertheless, until these 
amendments are formally adopted by the commission, the current 
version of Regulation 18901 remains in force. 
Our analysis in 
this letter is based upon the current version of this regulation. 
If you have any further questions regarding this matter 
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:JWW:plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
\( l 
~By: \John W. Wallace 
Counsel, Legal Division 


STATE 
P,O, BOX 942849 
SACRAMENTO, CA !lA?AIQ_""'" 
916-445-8188 
J\ssemhl~ 
nlifnrttin pregislnture 
COMMITTEES 
CHAIRMAN 
SELECT COMMITTEE ON 
OIL SPILL PREVENTION 
date: 
<date> 
To: 
<name> 
<address> 
TED LEMPERT 
ASSEMBLYMAN, TWENTIETH DISTRICT 
ECONOIVIIC UElVCUJt'MlcN AND 
NEW TECHNOLOGIES 
EDUCATION 
Recently, the State Legislature concluded the first half 
of a two-year legislative session. 
Enclosed you will find a list that will bring you up to 
date on the new laws that may have impact on you as a 
mobilehome park resident. 
This brief summary of legislation will hopefully be 
helpful. 
enclosure 


AB 212 
AB 416 
AB 1914 
SB 81 
SB 175 
1989 MOBILEHOME LEGISI~TION 
MOBILEHOME PARKS-PET POLICY --
Requires mobilehome parks to allow residents to keep 
their pets after the park adopts a no pet policy. 
Chapter 42. 
RESIDENT PURCHASE OF PARK EXE}1PT E'ROM 
SUBDIVISION MAP ACT --
Allows residents to apply for an exemption from Map 
Act requirements, if the tenants are seeking to buy a 
park in the form of a "condominium pa.rk purchase". 
Chapter 831. 
RIGHTS OF HEIRS AND JQINT rrENAN'fS --
Clarifies that joint owners j 
as well as heirs, have 
the right to resell a mobilehome following the death 
of the owner. 
Chapter 714. 
EXTENSION AND CHANGE OF DUTIES --
Extends the Mobilehome Ombudsman Program and allows 
the Ombudsman to provide assistance and take 
complaints on rent and lease issues, as well as 
provide information on such issues. 'I'lle measure also 
specifies that the Ombudsman may no"t mediate, 
negotiate, or provide legal advice on rent and lease 
issues. 
Chapter 189. 
HOMEOWNER BILL OF RIGHTS --
Prohibits park management from denying mobilehome 
owners the right to peacefully assemble, hold 
meetings, circulate petitions, and canvass mobilehome 
residents in the park at reasonable times and in a 
reasonable manner. 
Chapt.er 198.

sections.conclusion: This letter confirms that the sample of the mass mailing (copy attached) submitted with your request for confirmation does not violate the mass mailing provisions of the Act. Your mailing falls within the exception provided by Regulation 18901(e) because the elected officer's name appears only in the standard letterhead of the agency stationery, forms or envelopes.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a mass mailing from Assemblyman Ted Lempert's office containing his name and title in standard letterhead violate the Political Reform Act's mass mailing restrictions on public expense mailings?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the mass mailing does not violate section 89001 because the legislator's name appears only in the standard agency letterhead, which falls within an established exception to the mass mailing prohibition rules.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion. Document includes sample mass mailing about mobilehome legislation. FPPC notes pending regulation amendments but analysis based on current Regulation 18901. No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers but content clearly structured as request and response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does a mass mailing from Assemblyman Ted Lempert's office containing his name and title in standard letterhead violate the Political Reform Act's mass mailing restrictions on public expense mailings?

CONCLUSION: This letter confirms that the sample of the mass mailing (copy attached) submitted with your request for confirmation does not violate the mass mailing provisions of the Act. Your mailing falls within the exception provided by Regulation 18901(e) because the elected officer's name appears only in the standard letterhead of the agency stationery, forms or envelopes.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that a mass mailing from Assemblyman Lempert's office containing his name and title in standard letterhead does not violate the Political Reform Act's mass mailing restrictions. The mailing qualifies for an exception because the elected officer's name appears only in the standard stationery letterhead of the agency.
